reasoning: The FrontierMath benchmark was designed to be exceptionally challenging, with models originally scoring <2%. Since then, advances in search/reasoning-based models (e.g., o3, o3-mini, GPT-5.2 Pro) have pushed the state-of-the-art to roughly 31-32% as of August 2025. Given that FrontierMath consists of problems that take expert mathematicians hours to days to solve, progressing from ~30% to >80% requires an unprecedented leap in fundamental reasoning abilities within just four months. The current trajectory indicates diminishing returns as models tackle the most complex tiers (e.g., Tier 4). Therefore, I assign a 5% probability accounting for the possibility of an unexpected leap via major formal verification breakthroughs (e.g. specialized theorem provers).
